D11 ComputationalL3 ApplyReference

Blue-Noise Dither — Stipple-Like Patterning

Blue noise = high-frequency, low-correlation pattern; visually pleasing because the human visual system suppresses high frequencies more than low

Mitchell void-and-cluster (1991) generates a precomputed mask whose energy concentrates at high spatial frequencies. The result looks like stippling (Seurat-style) rather than crosshatching. Used in real-time global illumination, light-attenuating projections, and high-end print halftones.

Slide levels.
